perExample.coreMechanic: User holds a multi-file project (preview.html + config.json + overlay.js + several SVG layer files) in an in-memory virtual FS, edits files in tabs, and types a natural-language goal. On "Diff & apply" it runs gptdiff generateDiff over the whole project then smartapply to rewrite/add/remove files; the result is bundled (markers + script/asset inlining) into one self-contained HTML rendered in a sandboxed looping iframe (GSAP timeline animating SVG), optionally composited over a live webcam.
perExample.feedForward: no — core is a real gptdiff generateDiff then smartapply over a mutable multi-file virtual FS feeding a custom code-executing iframe preview (GSAP/SVG/webcam) and a bundle exporter; none of that is a plain text/llm/image DAG.
perExample.reasoning:
The overlay example's defining mechanic is runtime gptdiff over a mutable multi-file virtual FS rendered in a custom code-executing iframe — confirmed by reading both files. overlay.html (lines 189-190, 747-768) imports buildEnvironment/generateDiff/smartapply from gptdiff-js and at RUN time calls generateDiff(buildEnvironment(files), goal) then smartapply(diff, files) to rewrite/add/remove the user's own project files (preview.html + config.json + overlay.js + SVG layers), then bundles them into a sandboxed iframe (line 168: sandbox="allow-scripts") that executes a GSAP timeline animating inlined SVG, optionally composited over a getUserMedia webcam, plus a self-contained HTML bundle export and localStorage persistence with one-level undo.

nanoodle's play.html (lines 290-293, 882, 935-946) confirms the opposite shape: (1) gptdiff is vendored but used ONLY at app-BUILD time to restyle the app's OWN shell (index.html + app.css) — never as a runtime node on user content; (2) RUN is strict topological DAG execution that errors on "cycle detected" (line 882) — no loops, no persistent mutable state, no undo; (3) the node catalog is purely media/text generation (text/llm/image/edit/vision/tvideo/ivideo/vedit/lipsync/music/tts/transcribe) with NO node that maintains a multi-file map, executes arbitrary HTML/JS/SVG, runs GSAP/canvas, captures a webcam, or exports a binary bundle.

Every load-bearing piece of overlay is absent: runtime gptdiff (build-time only), multi-file VFS (single-graph only), code-executing iframe preview (no such node; only text/image/audio/video sinks), GSAP/SVG animation rendering, webcam source, self-contained-bundle binary export, and persistent state/undo (each run is stateless and feed-forward). An llm node could draft SVG/JS text, but nanoodle has no way to bundle, execute, or live-preview that text as an animated transparent overlay — so even the reduced essence ("see your overlay animate and edit it iteratively") cannot be surfaced. This is not a partial-fidelity case; the core is structurally impossible today.
missing: Runtime gptdiff on user content (generateDiff + smartapply over a project), with separately selectable diff and apply models — nanoodle only runs gptdiff at app-BUILD time on the app's own shell, never as a runtime node
Mutable multi-file virtual filesystem (a files map gptdiff can add/remove/rename, tab editing) — nanoodle has only one immutable graph and no file-collection data type

Custom code-executing live preview: a sandboxed iframe whose srcdoc bundles HTML/JS/SVG and runs a GSAP timeline in an infinite loop — nanoodle has no code-exec/HTML-preview node and no animation/canvas renderer
Webcam / getUserMedia live video source for compositing under a transparent overlay — no live-capture source node exists (upload/aupload/vupload are file pickers, not live streams)

Binary / self-contained bundle export (overlay-bundle.html with all assets inlined via Blob) for OBS — nanoodle only exports its own app as one .html; there is no per-run artifact bundler/downloader node
Persistent mutable state across runs plus one-level undo (files map + undoSnapshot, localStorage) — nanoodle RUN is stateless feed-forward DAG execution
Iterative edit loop / verify step — nanoodle errors on cycles ('cycle detected'); no loops, retries, or feedback edges
perExample.verifyNotes: Verified against the actual nanoodle source. play.html line 882 confirms strict topological DAG execution that errors on "cycle detected" (no loops/feedback/iteration/undo at runtime). Lines 2181/2196/2203 confirm gptdiff (buildEnvironment/generateDiff/smartapply) operates ONLY on APP_STATE.files["index.html"] and ["app.css"] — the generated app's own shell at BUILD time — never on user content and never as a runtime node. INPUT_SPECS (line 935) constrains the auto-derived form. The node catalog is purely media/text generation; there is no node for a multi-file VFS, code-executing iframe (overlay needs sandbox iframe + GSAP/SVG render), getUserMedia webcam compositing, binary self-contained-bundle export, or persistent mutable state. overlay's defining mechanics (runtime gptdiff over mutable multi-file project, executing animated SVG/GSAP overlay over webcam, bundle export, localStorage + one-level undo) are each structurally absent. No false-negative path: an llm node can emit SVG/JS as text, but nanoodle cannot bundle/execute/animate/composite/live-preview it, so even the reduced essence cannot be surfaced. Note: overlay.html itself is not present in this repo (it is the example being judged, external to nanoodle), consistent with the prior agent citing its line numbers separately. Correctly produced no graph.

perExample.coreMechanic: User edits one of several project files (geometry/material/SVG texture/effects/scene.json) and/or types a natural-language goal; gptdiff generateDiff produces a unified diff over the whole virtual file set, smartapply rewrites/adds/removes files, and the result is re-bundled into a single HTML doc rendered in a sandboxed iframe by Three.js. The viewer is orbit-controllable and can export the live mesh to .glb/.obj.
perExample.feedForward: no — core requires a virtual multi-file FS edited in place by gptdiff diff+smartapply at runtime, plus a custom code-executing Three.js iframe preview and a binary .glb export; none of these fit a loop-free {text,llm,image,...} DAG.
perExample.reasoning:
The object3d example's defining mechanic is a runtime gptdiff loop over a persistent virtual MULTI-FILE project (object.js / material.js / texture.svg / effects.js / scene.json), where generateDiff emits a unified diff over the whole file set and smartapply rewrites/adds/removes files in place, after which a CUSTOM BUNDLER stitches the files into one HTML doc and runs it inside a sandboxed Three.js iframe (importmap CDN, OrbitControls render loop) that can binary-export the live mesh to .glb/.obj. Checking the constraints one by one against what nanoodle can do today:

(1) Multi-file editable project: the node catalog has no "file set" / virtual-FS concept. text nodes are single strings, and the app builder's INPUT_SPECS (confirmed at play.html:935-946) only surface text.text, upload.image, aupload/vupload, llm.prompt/system, image.prompt, tvideo.prompt, music.prompt, tts.prompt — single scalar knobs, never a path->content map.

(2) Runtime gptdiff: ground truth and the file are explicit — gptdiff-js in play.html is used ONLY at app-BUILD time to restyle/relayout the generated app's OWN shell. There is no diff/smartapply runtime node, so "produce a unified diff over files and apply it" cannot be expressed. An llm node can emit text, but it cannot patch an existing file set in place; it would only re-emit a whole blob, and there is nowhere structured to hold that blob as N files.

(3) Custom code/3D preview: there is no code-execution / iframe / canvas / WebGL / Three.js preview node. The OrbitControl live render is the heart of "3D object studio" and has no representation.

(4) Binary export: outputs are limited to text/image/audio/video sinks. There is no .glb/.obj (ArrayBuffer Blob) export path. The media "save" only downloads CDN bytes for the four media types.

(5) Persistent mutable state + debounced live re-render: RUN is a single topological pass with cycles forbidden ("cycle detected", play.html:882). There is no files map persisted across runs, no active-tab state, no undo snapshot, no 450ms debounced re-render loop.

Even a heavily reduced version fails to capture the CORE: the most you could build is "type a goal -> llm writes a Three.js HTML string -> show it as text", but you could not RUN that HTML, could not orbit it, could not edit individual project files via diff, and could not export a mesh. That reproduces none of the four pillars (multi-file diff editing, live 3D, binary export, persistent state), so it is not even a meaningful partial — it is a different, far weaker thing. Verdict: not-buildable.
missing: Virtual multi-file project state (a path->content map of object.js/material.js/texture.svg/effects.js/scene.json) that can be edited, with files added/removed — nanoodle has only single-string text nodes and scalar form knobs.

Runtime gptdiff: generateDiff (produce a unified diff over the whole file set from a natural-language goal) + smartapply (rewrite/add/remove files in place). gptdiff is build-time-only and there is no diff/apply runtime node.

A code-executing live preview: sandboxed iframe running a stitched Three.js + OrbitControls WebGL render loop with importmap CDN deps, SVG-as-data-URL texture, and injected window.SCENE_CONFIG. No code/canvas/3D/iframe preview node exists.
A custom bundler that inlines local <script src> files and resolves include-config/include-texture markers into one runnable HTML doc.
Binary mesh export (.glb via GLTFExporter as ArrayBuffer Blob, .obj via OBJExporter) produced in-iframe and downloaded. Outputs are limited to text/image/audio/video sinks; no binary export.
Persistent mutable state across edits (files map, active tab, single-level undo snapshot) plus a debounced (450ms) live re-render. RUN is a single stateless topological pass and cycles are an error.
Two independently selectable models (diff model vs apply model) bound to the diff/apply steps.
perExample.verifyNotes: Verified against actual source. INPUT_SPECS (play.html:935-946) confirmed: only scalar string knobs (text.text, upload/aupload/vupload, llm.prompt/system, image.prompt, tvideo.prompt, music.prompt, tts.prompt) — no path->content map / virtual-FS. gptdiff's generateDiff/smartapply (play.html:2064-2229) operate ONLY at build time over the app's own index.html+app.css shell (CUSTOMIZE LOOP and PORT); there is no runtime diff/smartapply node. NODE_TYPES catalog (index.html:630-858) is exactly the 16 listed types — none is code-execution, file-set, 3D/WebGL/Three.js, OrbitControls preview, or binary export. The sole iframe (play.html:246) is the sandboxed app-preview shell, not a user-wirable code-run node. RUN is single topological pass; cycles error out (play.html:882), so no persistent files map / debounced live re-render. All four defining pillars (multi-file diff editing, live 3D orbit, .glb/.obj binary export, persistent mutable state) are structurally unrepresentable. The maximal reduction (llm emits a Three.js HTML string shown as text) reproduces zero pillars — not even a meaningful partial. Verdict stands: not-buildable, no graph correctly produced.

perExample.coreMechanic: User edits a multi-file JSON project (config.json with style/palette/character-bible + one panels/*.json per panel) and types a freeform goal; gptdiff generateDiff then smartapply rewrites all files (adding/removing/changing panels). The harness then deterministically composes every panel into ONE text prompt and renders the whole comic page as a single image; result is cached per prompt hash and exportable as PNG.
perExample.feedForward: partial — the AI calls form a clean DAG (upload/edit project -> llm diff -> edit/apply -> deterministic compose -> image), but it genuinely runs gptdiff smartapply over a multi-file virtual FS, which is a non-trivial multifile mutation step rather than a single text/edit primitive.
perExample.reasoning:
The comic's RENDER core is a clean feed-forward DAG and is expressible: gather structured comic spec (style + palette + character bible + per-panel beats) -> have an llm deterministically-ish compose ONE whole-page image prompt -> render the entire page as a single image. nanoodle does this natively: text source nodes feed an `llm` "composer" whose text output is wired into an `image` node's inline `prompt` field port, producing one whole-page render. The app builder auto-exposes every un-wired text/llm.prompt source as a form field and shows the image sink as the output, and export gives a self-contained .html whose rendered image can be saved (the comic's PNG export = the saved image). So the *output artifact* and the *compose-then-single-render* pipeline reproduce faithfully.

What is NOT reproducible, and why this is "partial" not "buildable": the comic's DEFINING mechanic is not the render — it's the multi-file virtual filesystem (config.json + one panels/*.json per panel, file tabs with add/delete) mutated at RUNTIME by gptdiff (generateDiff -> smartapply over USER content) from a freeform goal, with single-level undo and persistent state across edits. nanoodle explicitly has NO runtime gptdiff, NO multi-file virtual FS, NO add/remove-panel-as-files, and NO persistent mutable state across runs (each Run is a fresh topological pass; cycles are an error). gptdiff in play.html is build-time only (it restyles the app's own shell, never user content). The deterministic composePagePrompt (style+palette+only-the-cast-actually-used+per-panel shot/scene/caption/dialogue/fx with a 3000-char trim) can only be APPROXIMATED by an llm composer node (a chat model arranging the parts in reading order), not reproduced as pure JS. Per-prompt-hash caching and the "new take" nonce also have no node equivalent. Because the heart of the app (edit-a-living-multi-file-comic-project-with-AI-diffs) cannot run, but a meaningful reduced version (fill a structured form -> AI composes -> one whole-page comic render) genuinely works, the honest verdict is partial.
perExample.whatIsLost: Lost vs the original: (1) The multi-file virtual filesystem — config.json + one panels/*.json per panel, file tabs, add-panel via prompt(), delete via confirm(). Here panels are just lines in one textarea, not addressable files. (2) Runtime gptdiff editing — the original lets you type a freeform GOAL ("make panel 3 a night scene, add a panel where Pixel speaks") and gptdiff generateDiff+smartapply rewrites the project files (add/remove/change panels) over USER content; nanoodle has no runtime diff/apply node, so the user must hand-edit the spec text instead of steering it in natural language. (3) Deterministic JS composition (composePagePrompt: pulls only the cast actually used per panel, fixed reading order, 3000-char trim that drops trailing panel lines) is replaced by an llm composer, so the prompt assembly is approximate/non-deterministic rather than exact. (4) Per-prompt-hash caching (unchanged re-renders are free) and the "new take" nonce (force an uncached fresh sample of the SAME page) have no node equivalent — every Run re-renders and re-bills, and there is no one-click "reroll same spec" that bypasses cache. (5) Single-level undo of project edits. The single whole-page render, the structured style/palette/character/panel inputs, and the PNG-able output ARE preserved.
missing: Runtime gptdiff: turn a freeform natural-language goal into edits over user content (the comic's core 'steer the project by talking to it' loop)
Multi-file / addressable-item project state: per-panel files (or array items) you can add/remove/reference individually, persisting across edits
Deterministic structured-compose primitive: assemble many fields (style, palette, only-used cast, ordered panels) into one prompt with a length cap, without an LLM and non-deterministically
Per-hash render caching + a 'reroll same inputs' (nonce) control so unchanged re-runs are free and variation is one click
Exposing edit.prompt / vision.q / per-item prompts as app-builder form inputs (currently not in INPUT_SPECS)
perExample.verifyNotes:
Verified graph node-by-node against index.html node catalog (lines 678-723) and play.html INPUT_SPECS (935-946) + run engine (index.html 1428-1444).

GRAPH VALIDITY: all 9 nodes use real types (text x4, join x3, llm, image) with correct fields. All 8 links are well-formed: l1-l4 target join's declared "a"/"b" text input ports; l5-l6 chain join->join; l7 wires n7.text -> n8.prompt (llm inline field port, text); l8 wires n8.text -> n9.prompt (image inline field port, text). No dangling links, every node id exists, every source outport is "text". Inline field-port override is real: run engine (index.html 1434-1440) builds fieldOverrides for any link whose to.port isn't a declared input and merges it into n.fields, so wiring into "prompt" works as claimed. DAG is acyclic (n1-4 -> n5,n6 -> n7 -> n8 -> n9); no cycle error.

APP BUILDER SURFACING: deriveInputs (play.html 947-959) exposes the 4 text.text knobs as textareas (none are link-fed). n8.prompt and n9.prompt are link-fed so correctly hidden (no spurious empty-prompt inputs). n8.system is optional and surfaces as an optional textarea — harmless. deriveOutputs (962-967) yields n9 (image sink, no outgoing link) as the shown output. So form-in -> AI-composes -> single whole-page image render genuinely works and exports as a self-contained .html with a saveable PNG.

VERDICT CHECK (no false positive): graph is sound and runs; not an over-claim.
VERDICT CHECK (no false negative): the comic's defining mechanic is a runtime multi-file virtual FS (config.json + panels/*.json) mutated by gptdiff generateDiff/smartapply over USER content, with persistent state + undo + per-panel add/delete. Confirmed gptdiff in play.html (imports line 292; loop 2064-2229) targets ONLY the app's own shell (index.html + app.css, line 1648 "editable virtual files ... with the FIXED workflow") at build/customize/port time — never user content, no runtime VFS, no diff node, no persistent cross-run state (each Run is a fresh topo pass, cycles are an error). The deterministic JS composePagePrompt + prompt-hash caching + "new take" nonce have no node equivalent and can only be approximated by an llm composer. No cleverer feed-forward DAG recovers the living-multi-file-AI-diff editor. So "partial" is correct: the render pipeline reproduces, the editing heart does not.

perExample.coreMechanic: Two interactions over a virtual multi-file persona (soul/mood/memory/portrait/talkingto/chat .md + config.json/brief.md/style.css). (1) "send" = streaming chat built from persona artifacts, appended to chat.md. (2) personality change = one gptdiff beat: generateDiff over the persona files (chat.md excluded, fed only as read-only context), then smartapply rewrites ANY files at once — mood, memory, soul, config.json, style.css (hot-reloads theme) and portrait.md (triggers face re-render). State persists to localStorage and travels in save/load/share-by-URL.
perExample.feedForward: no — the gptdiff diff/smartapply beat mutates a multi-file virtual FS in place, accumulating persistent chat/mood/memory state across beats, with the diff conditionally feeding back into a portrait re-render and live CSS self-restyle; not a loop-free single-pass DAG.
perExample.reasoning: Marvis's DEFINING mechanic is the gptdiff "continue" beat: one streaming generateDiff + smartapply pass that simultaneously rewrites a multi-file virtual FS (soul/mood/memory/config.json/style.css/portrait.md/chat.md) IN PLACE, with that state persisting across beats in localStorage and traveling via save/load/share-by-URL, and the diff conditionally feeding a portrait re-render and a live CSS self-restyle. Every one of those pillars is outside nanoodle's app-builder envelope. nanoodle confirms (play.html:292 build-time only import, play.html:882 "cycle detected") that gptdiff is exposed ONLY at app-BUILD time to restyle the generated shell — there is NO runtime diff/smartapply node, NO multi-file virtual FS, NO persistent mutable state across runs (each Run is a fresh topological single pass), NO loops/feedback edges, and the editor.js style#theme hot-reload is not user-reachable as a node. So the iterative, in-place, multi-file, self-evolving persona is NOT feed-forward expressible and cannot be reproduced faithfully. A meaningful REDUCED version IS buildable, however: a single-pass "talk to a character" app. The form takes who-the-character-is plus the user's message; an llm composes the in-character reply (with the persona injected as its system prompt and the message wired into its prompt), a second llm derives a portrait prompt describing the character's current look/mood, an image node renders the face, and a tts node voices the reply. That captures the felt experience of "address a being and get a reply + a face + a voice" but is stateless: no memory accumulates, no mood/soul/style mutate across turns, no self-restyle. The catalog supports every node and inline-field wire this needs (llm prompt/system are inline ports; image.prompt is fed by a link; tts.prompt fed by a link), and the app builder will auto-derive the two text inputs from the un-fed text source nodes.
perExample.whatIsLost: Everything that makes marvis "alive over time" is lost. (1) Persistence/memory: nanoodle Runs are stateless single passes, so chat history, accumulated memory, and evolving mood do not carry between turns — every message is answered cold with no recollection of prior ones. (2) Self-mutation: the gptdiff beat that rewrites soul/mood/memory/config.json simultaneously cannot run; the character cannot grow, change personality, or learn. (3) Live self-restyle: style.css hot-reload into the page theme (the RPG-HUD reskinning the being controls) has no runtime node — the app's look is fixed at build time. (4) Conditional portrait re-render driven by a diff to portrait.md is replaced by an unconditional fresh render every Run (no content-hash caching, no "new look" nonce). (5) Save/load/share-by-URL of the evolving persona snapshot is gone (nanoodle only exports the static app and shares the graph, not per-session character state). The reduced app keeps only the single-beat illusion: address a described character, get a reply + a face + a voice, once.
missing: Runtime gptdiff (generateDiff + smartapply) as an executable node — currently gptdiff is build-time-only (play.html:292), so in-place multi-file rewriting cannot happen at app run time
A multi-file virtual filesystem object that a node can read and rewrite as a unit (soul/mood/memory/config.json/style.css/portrait.md/chat.md)
Persistent mutable state across Runs (localStorage-backed session) so memory/mood/chat-log accumulate — nanoodle Runs are independent single passes
Loop / feedback / iterative 'continue' beats — the executor treats cycles as an error ('cycle detected', play.html:882), so a being that advances each turn is impossible
Live CSS/theme self-restyle from generated content (inject style.css into a style#theme tag) reachable as a runtime node, not just the build-time shell restyle
Conditional / change-detected execution (re-render portrait only when portrait.md changed; nonce-forced 'new look') — no conditionals or content-hash caching at runtime
Save/load/share of per-session state (the evolving persona snapshot) — nanoodle only exports the static app and shares the graph
perExample.verifyNotes:
Verified against /home/ntc/dev/nanoodle/play.html. The "partial" verdict holds and the graph is valid.

NOT-BUILDABLE core confirmed: gptdiff is build-time only (no runtime diff/smartapply node); cycles are an error (topoOrder -> "cycle detected", play.html:882); each run does `graph.nodes.forEach(n=>{ n.out={}; })` (line 883) so there is zero cross-run persistent state; no multi-file virtual FS, no self-restyle node. So Marvis's iterative in-place self-evolving persona is genuinely outside the envelope. Correct to call it partial, not buildable.

REDUCED graph validates node-by-node:
- Inline field ports confirmed real: play.html:904-911 treats any link whose to.port is not a declared typed input as a fieldOverride merged into n.fields. So n1.text->n3.system, n2.text->n3.prompt, n1.text->n4.prompt, n4.text->n5.prompt, n3.text->n6.prompt are all legal text inline-port wires.
- Output ports correct: llm/text source/n4 emit `text` (717/693); image emits `image`; tts emits `audio`. All link from.ports are `text` (valid).
- No dangling endpoints, all node ids exist, feed-forward DAG (no cycle).
- INPUT_SPECS (935-946): n1.text and n2.text are un-fed text sources -> surfaced as the two form inputs as claimed. n3.system/n3.prompt/n5.prompt/n6.prompt are all link-fed -> hidden (deriveInputs line 953). n5 and n6 are sinks -> surfaced as image+audio outputs (deriveOutputs 962-967).

Two minor inaccuracies in the prior reasoning, neither affecting the verdict:
1. n4 (the portrait-prompt llm) has a typed-in `system` that is NOT link-fed, so per INPUT_SPECS llm.system(optional) it will ALSO surface as an extra OPTIONAL form field ("System prompt") with the typed value as default. The form therefore shows 2 required text inputs + 1 optional system field, not exactly two inputs. Harmless.
2. The character's reply text (n3) is not a sink (it feeds n6/tts), so the reply is delivered only as VOICE, not as visible text. The user sees a face (n5 image) and hears the reply (n6 audio) but does not read it. Still matches "reply + face + voice"; to also show the text one could add a second consumer or leave n3 as an additional sink.

Model ids (tts-1, seedream-v5.0-lite, zai/xiaomi ids) and fields (voice, size) pass through unvalidated at runtime, so they are not schema errors.

perExample.coreMechanic: User drops/captures ONE photo, toggles several "look" chips (and/or types a custom look), then hits Transform-each (renders every selected look in parallel via an image-edit model, each as a side-by-side tile) or Fuse (a chat model art-directs a single merged hybrid prompt from the selected chips, then renders N fresh variations of that one fused look). The same uploaded photo is cropped to the model's supported aspect and sent as the edit source for every render.
perExample.feedForward: partial — Transform-each is a clean fan-out of upload->image-edit over N looks, and Fuse is upload + chat(merge)->image-edit; both are feed-forward DAGs. But the per-look fan-out count is dynamic (user-selected chips/takes) and there's a runtime fallback branch (naiveFusion if chat JSON fails) plus per-tile regenerate, which a static DAG of fixed nodes doesn't cleanly capture.
perExample.reasoning: Core is a feed-forward DAG; gaps in whatIsLost and missingCapabilities.
perExample.whatIsLost: Lost: dynamic fan-out N (hardwired here), per-tile regenerate and naiveFusion fallback (no loops/conditionals), live model pickers (baked in), custom-look prompt-enhance input (edit.prompt not exposed), aspect auto-crop, share-looks URL. Kept: merge styles to one prompt then render, several restyles side-by-side.
missing: Runtime dynamic fan-out over user-selected items instead of fixed nodes
Multi-value chip input driving fan-out count
Per-output regenerate button
Live model picker in the built app
Auto crop image to model aspect ratio
Share-state-by-URL for app selections
perExample.verifyNotes:
Verified the graph node-by-node against index.html node catalog and play.html INPUT_SPECS/deriveInputs/deriveOutputs.

VALIDITY (no false positive):
- n1 upload []->image: source. Feeds n4/n5/n6 image ports (l3,l4,l5). upload.image is unfed -> app builder surfaces it as the "Image" form input. OK.
- n2 text []->text: text.text unfed -> surfaced as "Text" form input. Feeds n3.prompt (l1). OK.
- n3 llm: prompt fed by n2 (l1) -> hidden in form; system has a fixed value but is unfed+optional, so it WILL be surfaced as an optional "System prompt" field (harmless, pre-filled). Output text -> n4.prompt (l2). Confirmed at index.html:736 edit.run uses `inp.prompt ?? n.fields.prompt`, so prompt is a genuine wirable inline text port. OK.
- n4 edit: image(n1)+prompt(n3). Both fed. Output image, no outgoing link -> sink output. OK.
- n5/n6 edit: image(n1)+fixed prompt field. Output image, sink outputs. OK.
All link from-ports (text/image) and to-ports (image declared input; prompt inline field port) exist. No dangling links, no cycle — strictly feed-forward. nid=7/lid=6 consistent. No loop/diff/multifile/preview/binary-export required.

Result: 3 image outputs + 1 LLM-composed style from a text description + uploaded photo. Core "upload a portrait -> get stylized variants keeping identity/pose" is faithfully a feed-forward DAG the app builder can surface.

NO false negative either: a pictureme app's natural shape is a fan-out of N styles, which the catalog cannot do generically (no fan-out/grid-of-N node, no regenerate/iterate loop) — each style must be a hand-wired edit node (3 hardcoded here). That fixed-N / no-iteration limitation is exactly what 'partial' should capture; it is not actually 'buildable' (full) nor 'not-buildable'. Verdict partial stands.

perExample.coreMechanic: A reliability LOOP that counts a single integer from 1 to 100. Each step builds a virtual one-file environment {count: "N"}, calls gptdiff generateDiff with the goal "increment by exactly one", applies the diff via smartapply, then VERIFIES the parsed result equals before+1. The loop continues until it reaches 100 (pass), the first step that doesn't land on n+1 (fail), or the user presses Stop, logging cumulative token usage and cost throughout.
perExample.feedForward: no — the core is an unbounded sequential loop with a per-step verify (result==prev+1) that gates continuation, early-fail/early-stop, and mutable carried state (the running number), which a loop-free feed-forward DAG cannot express.
perExample.reasoning:
The defining mechanic of "count" is a reliability LOOP: starting from an integer, each step asks a model to "increment by exactly one" via runtime gptdiff (generateDiff + smartapply on a virtual one-file environment), then VERIFIES that the new parsed value equals before+1, and continues iterating until it hits 100 (pass), the first step that fails the +1 check (fail), or the user presses Stop — while accumulating token/cost across iterations. Four of nanoodle's hard constraints each independently kill this:

1) NO LOOPS / NO ITERATION. nanoodle's runtime (index.html topoOrder + play.html Run) is a single-pass topological evaluation of a feed-forward DAG. Any cycle is flagged "cycle detected" and errored (index.html:1413, play.html:882). The count loop is unbounded (up to 100 sequential steps, each depending on the previous result) — there is no way to express N sequential dependent iterations in a loop-free DAG, and certainly not an unbounded/data-dependent count of them.

2) NO PER-STEP VERIFY/GATE. The core is a guard: parse result, check /^-?\d+$/ AND next===before+1, and only then continue (else fail-stop). nanoodle has no conditional/branch/verify/retry node; execution can't gate continuation on a comparison.

3) NO RUNTIME gptdiff / smartapply. gptdiff-js is vendored in play.html but used ONLY at app-BUILD time to restyle the generated app's own shell. It is not a runtime node; the built app never runs generateDiff/smartapply on user content. The count example imports buildEnvironment/generateDiff/parseDiffPerFile/smartapply/callLlmForApply/setEnv at runtime (count.html). No equivalent node exists in the catalog.

4) NO PERSISTENT MUTABLE STATE across steps. The carried integer, the cumulative cost/token accumulator, and the early-stop are mutable state threaded across iterations. A feed-forward DAG carries no mutable state between (nonexistent) iterations.

The only thing the node catalog could produce is a single llm node prompted to "print 1..100", which has NONE of the load-bearing properties (no real per-step increment-by-diff, no exact +1 verification, no first-bad-step detection, no Stop-mid-flight, no unbounded reliability loop, no cumulative cost meter). That is a different artifact, not a reduced version of this one, so this is not even an honest "partial". Verdict: not-buildable.
missing: Bounded/unbounded ITERATION: the ability to run a step repeatedly N times (or until a condition), with each iteration depending on the previous iteration's output — impossible in a single-pass feed-forward DAG where cycles are an error.
A per-step VERIFY / CONDITIONAL GATE that compares a computed value against an expected value (next === before+1, regex shape check) and decides pass / fail-stop / continue.
EARLY-STOP / abort control: detect first-bad-step and halt, or let the user press Stop to cut an in-flight call (AbortController) and end the loop.

Runtime gptdiff editing: a node that, given a target value/'file' and a natural-language goal ('increment by one'), produces a diff and applies it (generateDiff + smartapply) at app-run time, not just at app-build time.
PERSISTENT MUTABLE STATE carried across iterations (the running integer) plus a CUMULATIVE ACCUMULATOR (running token/cost total) and a live per-step meter.

A LOOP TELEMETRY / log surface: per-iteration cost/token readout from x_nanogpt_pricing on the stream, with running total — the app builder today only shows terminal sink outputs, not a streamed iteration log.
perExample.verifyNotes: Verified against source. (1) play.html topoOrder (line 835) is single-pass Kahn's algorithm; leftover nodes are flagged cyclic -> "cycle detected" (line 882). No loop/iteration construct exists; runGraph clears n.out={} each run (line 883) so there is no persistent mutable state. (2) NODE_TYPES (lines 691-820) holds exactly the 16 catalog nodes; none branches, compares, verifies, retries, accumulates, or runs gptdiff. (3) gptdiff (generateDiff/smartapply, imported line 292) is used only by the build-time CUSTOMIZE LOOP (line 2064) operating on APP_STATE.files (the app's own index.html/app.css shell, MOUNT_IDS/CONTRACT about restyling) -- it is not a runtime node. (4) INPUT_SPECS (lines 935-946) matches ground truth. The "count" example needs an unbounded/data-dependent sequential reliability loop, runtime per-step gptdiff increment, exact +1 verify gating continuation, first-bad-step detection, mid-flight Stop, and a cumulative cost accumulator threaded across iterations -- each independently killed by no-loops / no-verify-gate / no-runtime-gptdiff / no-persistent-state. The only DAG-expressible approximation (one llm told to print 1..100) drops every load-bearing property, so it is not an honest partial. No graph is the correct output. Prior not-buildable verdict stands.

rootGaps.gap: G1 — Runtime gptdiff / instruct-edit (steer content by natural language at app-run time)
rootGaps.why: gptdiff (generateDiff + smartapply) is vendored in play.html (line 292) but is wired exclusively into the BUILD-TIME composer loop that rewrites the app's OWN shell — index.html + app.css (lines 256, 2064, 2166, 2229). There is no runtime node that, given a document/value plus a freeform goal, returns the rewritten content. Five of six examples are defined by 'type a goal, AI rewrites my stuff'. nanoodle's llm node can only emit fresh text from a prompt; it cannot patch existing content in place, so the entire 'steer by talking' interaction is absent at runtime.

rootGaps.gap: G2 — Multi-file / multi-item project state (a path->content map editable as a unit)
rootGaps.why: Four examples carry a virtual filesystem (overlay: preview.html+config+overlay.js+SVGs; object3d: object/material/texture/effects/scene; comic: config.json + one panels/*.json per panel; marvis: soul/mood/memory/config/style.css/portrait/chat). nanoodle has only single-string text nodes and a closed scalar INPUT_SPECS allowlist (play.html:935); there is no 'files' data type that can flow between nodes, no add/remove-item, no tabbed multi-file field. Without it there is nothing for a runtime gptdiff node to read and rewrite as a set.

rootGaps.gap: G3 — Code-executing live preview (sandboxed iframe that runs bundled HTML/JS/SVG)
rootGaps.why: overlay and object3d render their result by stitching the file set into one HTML doc and running it in a sandbox=allow-scripts iframe (GSAP timeline over SVG; Three.js + OrbitControls WebGL loop). nanoodle's outputs are limited to text/image/audio/video sinks — there is no node that executes arbitrary code or shows a live animated/interactive surface, and no bundler that inlines local script/asset references.

rootGaps.gap: G4 — Loop + verify (bounded sequential iteration with a per-step gate and early stop)
rootGaps.why: RUN is a single-pass topological evaluation and any cycle is a hard error ('cycle detected', index.html:1413 / play.html:882). count is an unbounded reliability loop with an exact result==prev+1 verify and Stop; marvis advances a persona one beat at a time; overlay re-applies edits iteratively. None of N-sequential-dependent-iterations, a conditional gate, or an abortable early-stop can be expressed in a loop-free DAG.

rootGaps.gap: G5 — Persistent mutable state across runs (carry/accumulate between executions)
rootGaps.why: Every Run is a fresh stateless topological pass; nothing carries over. count threads a running integer and a cumulative cost accumulator; marvis persists an evolving persona to localStorage and shares it by URL; comic keeps an undo snapshot and a per-prompt-hash image cache; overlay/object3d keep a files map + one-level undo. nanoodle exports/shares the static app and the GRAPH, never per-session runtime state, so memory, accumulation, caching and undo are all impossible.

rootGaps.gap: G6 — Dynamic fan-out of N (one parallel call per user-selected item)
rootGaps.why: pictureme's Transform-each maps the uploaded photo over N user-selected look chips in parallel (Promise.all), and Fuse renders 1/2/4/8 takes; comic can add/remove an arbitrary number of panels. A static DAG of fixed nodes cannot expand to a user-chosen count, so the grid-of-N and add-an-item behaviors collapse to a single hardwired output.

rootGaps.gap: G7 — Binary / bundle artifact export (download something other than the 4 media types)
rootGaps.why: overlay exports a self-contained overlay-bundle.html (all assets inlined via Blob); object3d exports .glb/.obj meshes (ArrayBuffer Blob from GLTFExporter/OBJExporter); marvis exports a gzipped JSON persona snapshot. nanoodle export is one .html of the app itself, and the media 'save' only downloads CDN bytes for image/audio/video. There is no per-run artifact bundler/downloader.

proposedFeatures.name: instruct node (runtime gptdiff, single-document)
proposedFeatures.shape: New node type instruct: inputs doc:text + goal:text (goal is an inline field port so it can be fixed or wired); fields diffModel and applyModel (NanoGPT chat ids, default xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro-ultraspeed); outputs text (rewritten document) and diff:text (the unified diff, for optional display). Internally wraps a {doc} one-entry env through the already-vendored buildEnvironment -> generateDiff(goal) -> smartapply, i.e. exposes the build-time machinery (play.html:292) as a runtime node. INPUT_SPECS gains instruct.goal so the app form auto-shows a single 'What do you want to change?' box.
proposedFeatures.easeOfUse: One box plus Run. No diff syntax, no patch hunks, no filesystem. It is just another chat-style node with a goal textarea and two model dropdowns (sensible defaults, can stay hidden). Reuses code already in play.html, so it stays inside the single-static-file ethos.

proposedFeatures.name: project (files) type + tabbed multi-file field
proposedFeatures.shape: Add a 'files' data type (a map path->content) to the port system so a project flows between nodes. A project source node holds a tabbed file editor (add/delete tab); each file path is ALSO an individually wirable text port (file['scene.json'] etc.) so an llm/instruct output can populate one file. The instruct node gains a project-mode variant: inputs project:files + goal:text -> project:files (generateDiff/smartapply over the whole set, add/remove/rename included). App builder renders each file as its own labeled textarea, so simple apps never open the tabs.
proposedFeatures.easeOfUse: For the common case the user edits friendly per-file textareas (Geometry / Material / Scene; or Style / Panels) and presses Run — no paths, no code-as-string juggling. The files type is mostly plumbing that lets instruct and preview nodes connect; advanced users get tabs, beginners get a form.

proposedFeatures.name: state node (persistent slot, deferred-write)
proposedFeatures.shape: New node type state: input in:text (value to remember) -> output out:text (the value from the PREVIOUS run), backed by localStorage keyed by node id. The executor READS the stored value at the start of a Run (so downstream gets last-run's value without forming a cycle) and COMMITS in:text at the end of the Run. This is a deferred write, not a feedback edge, so cycle detection (index.html:1413 / play.html:882) is untouched. A built-in 'reset' and an optional cache-by-hash mode (return cached artifact for identical inputs; a 'New take' nonce forces a fresh sample) cover comic's per-hash cache + reroll.
proposedFeatures.easeOfUse: Drop a node, wire reply in / memory out. The app surfaces a quiet 'Memory' the user never configures — they just keep interacting and the app remembers. Reset and 'New take' are single buttons. Pairs with Session save/load/share so the evolving state travels by URL using nanoodle's existing #a= plumbing.

proposedFeatures.name: loop node (bounded iterate-until) + check/gate node
proposedFeatures.shape: loop is a container node: port init:any seeds the body; an inner single-node body slot whose output the ENGINE threads back as the next iteration's input (engine unroll, not a user-drawn cycle, so cycle detection stays intact); fields maxIters and an optional 'until' predicate; outputs final value + iteration count, plus a streamed run-log. check/gate node: inputs value:text + expected:text, field mode = equals | regex | numeric-+1, outputs pass:text (passes value only when predicate holds) and fail:text; a fail sets the loop's stop flag, giving the pass/fail-stop/Stop trichotomy and an AbortController-backed Stop button.
proposedFeatures.easeOfUse: User drags ONE node into the loop body, sets max=100, wires a seed and a result. The gate is a dropdown with two output dots. No expressions for common numeric/equality cases; the live iteration log + Stop button appear automatically when a loop exists. Normal graphs are unaffected — the runtime special-cases only this node.

proposedFeatures.name: fanout (map) node + chip multi-select input
proposedFeatures.shape: fanout node: inputs list:text (one item per line) + an optional typed image:image input + a templated prompt with an {item} token -> emits an array surfaced as a tile grid (N parallel calls in one forward pass, no cycle). A companion app-builder flag kind=chips on a text source renders preset toggle chips that join into the list text. Optional per-tile regenerate re-runs only that tile's sub-DAG.
proposedFeatures.easeOfUse: Wire upload -> fanout, type looks one per line or tick chips; the grid appears with no node duplication. Single forward pass, so it never trips cycle detection. Chips are plain text under the hood, feeding existing ports.

proposedFeatures.name: preview node (sandboxed code runner + bundler) with binary/bundle export
proposedFeatures.shape: preview node: input project:files (or html:text). Bundles the set (inline local <script src>, resolve include-config/include-texture markers, inject an importmap) into one srcdoc and renders it in a sandbox=allow-scripts iframe. Ships templates: a GSAP/SVG overlay template and a Three.js + OrbitControls template. It is a SINK (display), never feeding a cycle; an optional image port can capture a still frame for downstream. Pairs with a generalized download: an exporter in the iframe postMessages an ArrayBuffer (overlay-bundle.html, .glb, .obj) to the parent, which the app builder offers as a 'Download' button — generalizing the existing media 'save' to arbitrary blobs.
proposedFeatures.easeOfUse: The user just sees and orbits/plays the result; bundling and iframe plumbing are hidden, no server, no build step. Download is one more button in the generated app. This is the heaviest add and gates only two examples, so it ranks last.

synthesis.unlockMap:
Already buildable today (as honest PARTIALS, core render-pipeline only): comic (form -> llm composer -> single whole-page image), marvis (single-pass talk-to-a-character: persona as system + message -> llm reply -> portrait image + tts voice), pictureme (upload + chat-merge -> image-edit, a few restyles side-by-side). Not buildable today: overlay, object3d, count.

Then, in leverage order:
1. instruct node (G1, low complexity) -> upgrades comic and marvis from partial to full-fidelity steer-by-talking (freeform 'change this' rewrites the spec/persona), and makes the count STEP expressible (increment-by-one as a runtime edit). Touches 5/6 examples, ships by reusing vendored gptdiff. Highest leverage.
2. loop + check/gate (G4, medium) -> on top of instruct, fully unlocks count (bounded iterate + exact +1 verify + Stop). The one primitive that turns a sequential reliability loop into a buildable graph.
3. state node + cache/reroll + session save-load-share (G5, medium) -> fully unlocks marvis as 'alive over time' (accumulating memory/mood, shareable evolving persona) and gives comic per-hash cache + 'New take'. Memory across runs.
4. project (files) type + multi-file instruct (G2, medium) -> makes comic's add/remove-panel and marvis's true multi-file persona faithful; prerequisite plumbing for the preview node. Lifts the two partials to full fidelity.
5. fanout + chips (G6, medium) -> fully unlocks pictureme (dynamic grid-of-N over selected looks). Self-contained, unlocks exactly one example.
6. preview + bundler + binary export (G3+G7, high) -> the only path to overlay and object3d (live GSAP/SVG and Three.js, .glb/.obj/bundle download). Largest build, unlocks two; ships last.
synthesis.rankedRoadmap:
Smallest orthogonal set that unlocks the most, ranked by examples-unlocked-per-unit-complexity:

#1 instruct node (runtime gptdiff, single-doc) — complexity LOW, touches 5/6 examples, reuses code already vendored at play.html:292. Lifts comic + marvis from partial toward full, and provides count's per-step edit. BUILD FIRST — by far the best leverage.

#2 loop node + check/gate — complexity MEDIUM, fully unlocks count (the only fully not-buildable example reachable by a small primitive). Engine-unrolled body sidesteps the 'cycle detected' guard without weakening it for normal graphs.

#3 state node (+ cache/reroll + session save-load-share) — complexity MEDIUM, fully unlocks marvis's persistence/evolution and gives comic free re-renders + reroll. Reuses the existing #a= share plumbing.

#4 project (files) type + multi-file instruct mode — complexity MEDIUM, raises comic/marvis to full multi-file fidelity and is the prerequisite plumbing for #6.

#5 fanout (map) node + chip input — complexity MEDIUM, fully unlocks pictureme. Orthogonal, single-example payoff, but cheap and clean.

#6 preview node (sandboxed bundler) + binary/bundle export — complexity HIGH, the sole route to overlay and object3d (live code execution + .glb/.obj/bundle). Two examples, highest cost, lowest leverage — BUILD LAST.

After #1–#5, five of six examples are fully buildable; overlay and object3d require #6's code-executing preview, which is the only addition that genuinely strains the no-code, four-media-sink, no-arbitrary-execution ethos. If a line must be drawn, draw it before #6: ship #1–#5 and accept that live-code-preview studios stay out of scope.
synthesis.summary: No — not every example is buildable in nanoodle today. Exactly three (comic, marvis, pictureme) are buildable now, but only as honest PARTIALS: their feed-forward render core (structured form -> llm composer -> single image, or upload + chat-merge -> image-edit grid, or persona-as-system -> reply + face + voice) maps cleanly onto the node catalog and app builder, while their defining mechanic — steering content by natural language, persistent/evolving state, and dynamic fan-out — does not. Three (overlay, object3d, count) are not buildable at all. The misses cluster into 7 root gaps, dominated by one: there is no RUNTIME content-rewrite node — gptdiff is vendored but build-time-only (it rewrites the app's own shell at play.html:292, never user content), and RUN is a single stateless topological pass where cycles are a hard error (index.html:1413 / play.html:882). The highest-leverage fix is a single low-complexity 'instruct' node that exposes the already-vendored gptdiff machinery at runtime; it touches 5 of 6 examples. Adding a loop+gate, a persistent state node, a files type, and a fanout node (all medium complexity) lifts five of six examples to full fidelity. Only overlay and object3d need the one heavyweight, ethos-straining addition — a sandboxed code-executing preview with binary export — which should ship last, if at all.
